question.text: Under Section 6(2) of SCRA, for how long must members of a recognized stock exchange maintain and preserve books of accounts?

answer.text: For periods not exceeding 5 years as prescribed by the Central Government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 6(2) requires every member to maintain and preserve for periods not exceeding 5 years such books of accounts and other documents as the Central Government may prescribe after consultation with the concerned stock exchange. **The Trap:** The 5-year period is the maximum preservation period the government can prescribe — actual periods may be shorter. These records are subject to SEBI inspection. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Different documents have different preservation periods under SCRR — main books for 5 years, contract notes/memos for 2 years. Know the distinction.
